steps.title: Use the constant term property
steps.content: When multiplying $(2x - 5)$ and $(x + k)$, the constant term of the product equals the product of their constant terms. Since $2x^2 + x - 15$ has constant term $-15$: $$(-5) \cdot k = -15$$

steps.title: Solve for k and verify
steps.content:
Dividing both sides by $-5$: $$k = \frac{-15}{-5} = 3$$
Verify by expanding $(2x - 5)(x + 3)$: $$2x^2 + 6x - 5x - 15 = 2x^2 + x - 15$$
